summary: Accepted the F005 test baseline. Root Vitest/fast-check tests pass, existing web Vitest tests remain green, Storybook build and Playwright Chromium e2e have credible owner exit-0 evidence with configs/scripts inspected. Lead scope reconciliations are justified and no product, architecture, or business implementation drift was found.

tests_run.result: Prettier, ESLint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundaries passed.
Simulation determinism passed; root Vitest passed 1 file and 2 tests; web Vitest passed 1 file and 3 tests.
Reviewer-side probe was manually stopped after hanging; non-blocking because Storybook build dispatch path was inspected and owner evidence recorded pnpm storybook:build exit 0.

decisions: Accepted owner evidence for pnpm storybook:build and pnpm test:e2e --project=chromium because the strict read-only review avoided rerunning commands that write transient outputs under .tmp and test-results.

Accepted lead scope reconciliations for package.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, pnpm-workspace.yaml, tsconfig.json, and .gitignore because they are tied to root scripts, install/build approval, lint/typecheck inclusion, and test output hygiene.
risks: Playwright uses fixed local port 4173, so a conflicting process can fail e2e.
Storybook and e2e were accepted from owner evidence rather than rerun in the strict read-only review.
Vite emits the existing dynamic import analysis warning for apps/web/src/worker/hello-simulation-adapter.mjs during e2e.
requested_action: Lead may mark FOUNDATION-005 ACCEPTED and prepare integration.
